meaning: adj.偶像破坏的,打破旧习的
examples: It is crucial for success in any field to understand how the iconoclastic mind works.
At least so argues E. Pessen in his iconoclastic study of the very rich in the United States between 1825 and 1850.
Frank Field, an iconoclastic Labour MP, notes that benefits for single mothers penalise those in stable relationships, which are clearly associated with good parenting.
For all its great length, it miraculously retains the pace and exhilaration of an iconoclastic essay.
Archigram's witty and iconoclastic proposals included "Walking Cities" that moved, armadillo-like, with insouciance and self-sufficiency.
